scanPrerequisites.description: akita-plan must consume the persisted scan snapshot above and stop when it is missing instead of reconstructing repo facts from chat memory.

requiredMachineState.description: Canonical plan review packet. It must keep the active target, shortlist, backlog, lightweight review status, and unresolved blockers in one durable JSON file.
Frozen approved snapshot that may be written only after an explicit approve action. It must keep approved intent plus minimal audit metadata, not duplicate manifest or bundle truth.

shortlistPolicy.description: akita-plan aims for a human-reviewable shortlist of three to seven flows, but if fewer than three viable flows honestly exist it must record the shortage and stop with needs-review instead of padding the shortlist.

reviewActions.description: Approve the entire current shortlist and create the approved snapshot.
Approve only the selected shortlist ids and create the approved snapshot.
Reject selected shortlist ids without creating an approved snapshot.
Revise one shortlisted flow and return the shortlist to draft review.
Select a different active target and invalidate the current shortlist review.
Re-rank and regenerate the shortlist from persisted scan state.

reviewStatus.description: Lightweight review status only. Keep the current review state and the last explicit human action, not a transition table.

stopConditions.description: Required persisted scan state is missing, so planning cannot proceed honestly.
A manifest-listed capability bundle asset is missing, so planning must stop instead of guessing capability coverage.
Fewer than three viable flows exist for the active target, so the shortlist must not be padded.
The active target cannot be selected honestly from persisted scan state.

summaryState.description: Human-readable review packet only. Canonical machine state stays in the two JSON files above.
